finding: catalog.rs moved with ONLY two edition-2024 let-chains lowered to nested `if let` (the exact 2-term let-chain desugaring). policy.rs + tools.rs moved byte-for-byte (diff vs git HEAD empty, exit 0). control_mcp/mod.rs's `pub use ziee_control_mcp::{catalog,policy,tools}` aliases the modules so every super::/boot-site path is unchanged. ziee + ziee-desktop compile exit 0.

policy.rs (is_denied/is_mutating/DENY_PATH_PREFIXES/SSE+token+byte-stream guards) and tools.rs (3 tool descriptors + name consts) are byte-identical to the ziee originals: `diff <(git show HEAD:.../policy.rs) sdk/.../policy.rs` and the tools.rs equivalent both exit 0. No sort/text/logic change.

The JSON-RPC envelope types (JsonRpcRequest/Response/Error + constructors + consts) moved to ziee_framework::mcp verbatim; the sole production edit is `crate::common::AppError` -> `ziee_core::AppError` in from_app_error. In ziee crate::common::AppError IS a B1 re-export of ziee_core::AppError, so the status_code/error_code/to_string surface + the 400/403/404/409/410/422->invalid_params classification is identical. 5 envelope tests pass in the framework.

loopback_host moved verbatim: `pub fn loopback_host(_server_host:&str)->&str{"127.0.0.1"}` still ignores its arg and always returns loopback, preserving the invariant that no config-set server.host can redirect a built-in MCP server's JWT-bearing self-dial off-host (the attacker.com exfil regression). Both security regression tests moved to mcp.rs and pass. All 15 code_sandbox::loopback_host(...) callers resolve via the mod-level re-export.

SDK workspace is edition 2021 (B1..B6); ziee is 2024. catalog.rs used 2 let-chains (2024-only) that broke the verbatim move. Lowered to nested if-let (semantically identical short-circuit AND + early return) rather than bumping one crate to 2024, keeping the workspace uniformly 2021. `cargo check --workspace` exit 0; detects_secret_request_field (drives the exact lowered paths) passes.

ziee-control-mcp Cargo.toml adds aide(features axum/axum-json/macros)+serde_json 1.0.141+tracing 0.1, versions matching the ziee server catalog so the single src-app/Cargo.lock unifies (no duplicate build). server/Cargo.toml adds the ziee-control-mcp path dep. framework lib.rs `pub mod mcp;` + re-exports. cargo check -p ziee / -p ziee-desktop / --workspace all exit 0.

ziee-control-mcp names NO app type + NO DB: catalog=aide::openapi::OpenApi+serde_json+std+tracing; policy=serde_json+the sibling Operation; tools=serde_json. framework::mcp names only serde/serde_json/ziee_core::AppError. The mcp_servers row WRITE (repository.rs upsert) + the RequirePermissions/User/Group permission filter (handlers.rs) STAYED app-side, so the moved core is genuinely build-DB-free. skeleton-server (SDK-only) still checks.

The secret-body denylist recursion (schema_has_secret_field* + is_secret_field_name compounds/token-suffix/word-part rules + $ref/anyOf/oneOf/allOf resolution + the depth-6 cycle guard) moved intact; the let-chain lowering did not alter the traversal. detects_secret_request_field asserts direct/$ref/nested-object/array-items/nullable-anyOf detection + the false-positive guards (max_tokens/token_source/token_count) and passes.

The deployment-invariant reachability policy (auth-token/test/health/server-update prefixes, .../mcp last-segment recursion block, SSE-stream/token-mint/byte-stream/secret-body/multipart denials) moved byte-for-byte. denylist_covers_every_category + segment_matching_does_not_overreach pass. The per-USER permission filter (user_may_run/op_available/resolve_op, the forbidden==not-found no-probing rule) intentionally stayed in the app-side handler (names User/Group) — unchanged.

control_mcp::catalog::init_from_openapi (lib.rs:540, main.rs:347), super::catalog/policy/tools (handlers.rs), control_mcp::handlers::control_call_needs_approval (mcp/chat_extension/mcp.rs:2360, js_tool/executor.rs:149) all resolve unchanged. code_sandbox::types::JsonRpc* (13 handlers) + code_sandbox::loopback_host (15 modules) resolve via the two re-export shims. No public API of ziee changed (golden E8 confirms).

cargo test -p ziee-control-mcp => 11 passed (7 catalog: parses_operations_by_id/extracts_required_permission/no_permission_in_description_is_none/json_body_flag_distinguishes_multipart/path_params_extracted/detects_secret_request_field/parse_permission_edge_cases; 4 policy: is_mutating_only_non_get/denylist_covers_every_category/normal_operations_are_allowed/segment_matching_does_not_overreach). Moved with the code.

cargo test -p ziee-framework mcp:: => 7 passed (5 JSON-RPC envelope: round_trip/accepts_missing_jsonrpc_field/accepts_string_id/error_helpers_have_canonical_codes/response_serializes_with_either_result_or_error; 2 loopback: always_127_for_wildcards/pins_regardless_of_server_host). The 5+2 moved out of code_sandbox/types.rs + code_sandbox/mod.rs; the remaining flavor-lock + ConversationIdHeader + server_id tests stayed.

Dropped the now-unused serde::{Deserialize,Serialize} import from code_sandbox/types.rs (the JSON-RPC block was its only user) so -D warnings stays clean. Retained ziee-control-mcp's unused-in-v1 framework+identity deps (plan §1.2; allow-by-default lint). No NEW warnings: the only 4 ziee(lib) dead-code warnings (KB_MAX_DOCUMENTS_DEFAULT/is_unread/is_active/list_enabled_for_health_check) predate C1.

control_mcp/mod.rs: the three `pub mod catalog/policy/tools;` became one `pub use ziee_control_mcp::{catalog,policy,tools};`. A `pub use` of a module makes control_mcp::catalog an alias, so handlers.rs's `use super::catalog::{self,ControlCatalog,Operation}` + `catalog::build_catalog` (handler tests) + policy::/tools:: paths resolve. ControlCatalog+Operation cross the crate via already-pub methods/fields (no visibility bump).

code_sandbox/types.rs re-exports JsonRpcError/Request/Response from ziee_framework::mcp; code_sandbox/mod.rs re-exports loopback_host. Both are single `pub use` lines replacing the moved defs, so the 28 downstream call sites are untouched. ziee build green proves resolution.

E8 openapi.json: ui + desktop canonically-equal via `diff <(jq -S . baseline) <(jq -S . regen)` == empty on both. The moved pieces touch no route/schema/permission-string, and the JSON-RPC types are never JsonSchema (control route uses .route()), so only linkme dep-graph top-level key order churns (E8 REFINEMENT). Restored via git checkout.

The desktop combined (server+desktop) surface is unaffected: cargo check -p ziee-desktop exit 0; the desktop --generate-openapi run yields types.desktop.ts byte-identical (cmp) + openapi.desktop.json canonically-equal (jq -S) vs baseline. loopback_host/JsonRpc/control-core all reached through the same ziee lib the desktop crate embeds.
